original_hypothesis.title: acceptAddressesAndPercentages Allows Approval of Unproposed Zero-Address Destinations Burning Artist Funds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: acceptAddressesAndPercentages can set primary/secondary address status to true even when no proposal was made (all addresses default to address(0), all percentages default to 0). If payArtist is then called, ETH is sent to address(0) via .call which succeeds on Ethereum, permanently burning the artist's share of royalties.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The function has no check that proposePrimaryAddressesAndPercentages was actually called. Default values (zero addresses, zero percentages) pass the percentage validation in payArtist since 0 + teamperc1 + teamperc2 = 100 requires artistPercentage = 0. But if setPrimaryAndSecondarySplits set a non-zero artist percentage, then approval of zero-address proposals would cause ETH sent to address(0).

proof_of_signal.pattern: acceptAddressesAndPercentages directly sets status = _statusPrimary with no check that propose was called. Default primaryAdd1/2/3 = address(0). payArtist sends ETH to these addresses via .call which succeeds for address(0).

preconditions: Function admin calls acceptAddressesAndPercentages before proposal is made
setPrimaryAndSecondarySplits configured with non-zero artist percentage

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No require() checking proposedAddresses != address(0) in acceptAddressesAndPercentages
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin mistakenly or maliciously approves before proposal; payArtist sends to address(0)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Artist's share is permanently burned; team receives their share normally

economic_questions: Does .call{value: x}('') to address(0) succeed and burn the ETH?
Can this be triggered by admin error vs malicious intent?
Is there any off-chain process preventing premature approval?
validation_requirements: Test payArtist with zero artist addresses
Verify ETH is sent to and consumed by address(0)

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Entire artist share of collection revenue burned

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
